subject: 🔄 Z.ai GLM-5V-Turbo converts screenshots to runnable code
snippet: ⚡️ Z.ai turns UI screenshots into code with GLM-5V-Turbo Alibaba's Qwen3.6-Plus gets1M context, DeepMind says the web attacks AI agents, Claude Code can now smoothly render UI. Stay updated with today’s top AI news, papers, and repos. Signup | Work With Us | Follow on X Hey Aikka…
body_html:
Alibaba ships Qwen3.6-Plus with 1M context for multi-step coding, DeepMind exposes web-based agent manipulation attacks
Alibaba's Qwen3.6-Plus gets1M context, DeepMind says the web attacks AI agents, Claude Code can now smoothly render UI.
Stay updated with today’s top AI news, papers, and repos.
Signup | Work With Us | Follow on X

Hey Aikkara,

Your daily briefing is ready. You can finally take a break from the AI firehose.

Our algos spent the night splitting signal from noise and pulled the top news, models, papers, and repos.

Here’s the must-read:

Together with:
Summary

Read time: 4 min 31 sec

Top News

▸ Z.ai introduces GLM-5V-Turbo to convert screenshots into runnable code

Sonar

▸ Read how to replace manual checks with automated AI code verification

Top News

▸ Alibaba ships Qwen3.6-Plus for multi-step coding and tool-based agents

Datadog

▸ Learn how to detect and investigate issues using automated AI workflows

Top Paper

▸ Google shows how the web itself can hijack AI agents with Agent Traps

Signals

▸ Anthropic enables smoother Claude Code terminal UI with new renderer
▸ H Company unveils open computer-use models beating GPT-5.4 at lower cost
▸ Microsoft presents new speech model with lowest WER across 25 languages
▸ Alibaba releases unified image model for image generation and editing
▸ Together AI announces an open-source system that improves decoding over time
Today's Author
Lior Alexander. Founder of AlphaSignal and former ML Engineer. Previously built ML systems at Iguazio, Guesty, Enphase, Mila.
Top News
Z.ai launches GLM-5V-Turbo to convert images, videos, and layouts into structured, runnable code outputs
5,483 Likes

Z.ai launched GLM-5V-Turbo, a multimodal coding model that converts visual inputs into working code.

It takes screenshots, design drafts, videos, or documents and generates structured implementations. This targets the manual step where developers translate UI designs into code.

What it does

It connects visual understanding with code generation in one system.

  • Reads UI screenshots, design files, and layouts, then generates runnable frontend or backend code
  • Handles text, images, videos, and files together instead of separate processing pipelines
  • Extends tools to include multimodal search, drawing, and webpage reading capabilities

How it works

It combines vision and coding models through integrated training stages.

  • Uses CogViT encoder to interpret layout structure, objects, and spatial relationships
  • Trains with reinforcement learning across 30+ task types including GUI-based interactions
  • Builds synthetic environments to generate verifiable data for action prediction tasks

Accessibility

  • Available through web interface for direct testing with multimodal inputs
  • Provides API access for integration into coding tools and agent systems
EXPLORE GLM TURBO
Presented by Sonar
How to scale code reviews when AI outpaces you

AI-driven velocity is creating a verification bottleneck. To scale, teams must transition from manual checks to an automated, source-agnostic verification layer.

Key Insights

  • The Trust Gap: 96% of devs distrust AI output; 61% report "AI builds code that looks correct but isn't reliable."

  • Automated Gates: Moving from manual checks to automated, deterministic guardrails.

  • SDLC Integration: Treating AI as "trusted but verified" to secure the end product at any scale of development operations.
READ THE REPORT
partner with us
Top News
Alibaba releases Qwen3.6-Plus with 1M context, enabling multi-step coding agents across repositories and tools
2,528 Stars

Alibaba launches Qwen3.6-Plus, a hosted model built for agent workflows instead of single prompts.It targets agent workflows by combining reasoning, memory, and tool execution in one system.

The most notable capability is support for 1M token context alongside multi-step repository-level coding tasks. So you stop stitching context manually.

You can use it through API or plug it into tools like OpenClaw or Claude Code. It reads your repo, edits code, runs commands, and updates outputs across steps.

Features and results

  • 1M token context supports large repos, logs, and multi-step agent traces
  • SWE-bench Verified: 78.8, Terminal-Bench: 61.6 for execution-heavy workflows
  • DeepPlanning: 41.5 shows improved long-horizon task planning capability
  • LiveCodeBench v6: 87.1 for coding accuracy across real tasks
  • Multimodal input handles text, images, UI screenshots, and document parsing
TRY QWEN3.6
Presented by Datadog
Your Always-On AI Teammate

What if incident response started before your team even logged in?

Learn how Bits AI SRE automates investigations, connects logs and telemetry, surfaces likely root causes, and drafts summaries and follow-ups.
GET THE BRIEF
partner with us
Top Paper
Google DeepMind presents AI Agent Traps framework exposing how web content manipulates agent pipelines
2,392 Likes

Google DeepMind introduces AI Agent Traps, a framework that treats web content as a direct input that can manipulate agent behavior. Agents that browse, retrieve, and act on external data now face attacks embedded inside HTML, documents, and interfaces.

Hidden prompt injections influence agent actions in up to 86% of cases, and memory poisoning reaches 80%+ success with less than 0.1% corrupted data.

What this framework defines
It maps how attacks target different parts of an agent system.

  • Content Injection hides instructions inside HTML or CSS that agents still parse
  • Semantic Manipulation inserts misleading context that alters reasoning steps
  • Cognitive State attacks corrupt memory systems like RAG
  • Behavioural Control forces actions such as unintended API calls or data access

How these attacks work in practice
Agents trust external content without verifying intent or structure.

  • Retrieval systems surface adversarial documents due to semantic similarity matching
  • Parsers include hidden or off-screen content into the model input context
  • Execution layers trigger tool calls directly from corrupted instructions
TEST AGENT TRAPS
Signals
1 Anthropic introduces NO_FLICKER mode in Claude Code using a virtualized terminal renderer for smoother performance 9,258 Likes
2 H Company launches Holo3, open-weight computer-use models scoring 78.9% on real desktop tasks beating GPT-5.4 2,193 Likes
3 Microsoft releases MAI-Transcribe-1 with 3.9% WER, outperforming Whisper and Gemini across 25 languages 1,401 Likes
4 Alibaba presents Wan2.7-Image with interactive editing using visual instructions directly on images 1,219 Likes
5 Together AI unveils Aurora, an open-source system that updates itself during use instead of relying on offline retraining 1,023 Likes
